title: Andromeda
summary: A full mod + backend revival stack for Enemy on Board: Avalonia installer (Win/Linux), a from-scratch mod with zero docs, and dockerized game servers + Core REST API deployed on cost-optimized GCP with CI/CD releases and in-mod update warnings.

content.text: **Andromeda** is an end-to-end multiplayer revival stack for *Enemy on Board* — not just a mod. It combines **client patching**, a **cross-platform installer**, and a **production backend** into one coherent system.

#### Why it’s hard
Built with **0 official docs**: behavior was derived from the shipped game via **reverse engineering**. The goal wasn’t a toy server — it had to be **stable in production** for real players.

#### What ships together
- **Installer (Avalonia, C#)**: Windows + Linux. Installs prerequisites (incl. MelonLoader), downloads the correct mod from GitHub releases, and applies config. **Install time: ~5 seconds.**
- **Mod (C#)**: built from scratch; runtime patching + gameplay integration.
- **Core (REST API)**: coordination layer.
- **Dockerized game servers**: reproducible deployments.

#### Deployment
Runs on **GCP** with machines that are **suspended/started** to avoid idle costs. **GitHub CI/CD** drives production deployments. The mod is **update-aware** and warns users to prevent client/server version drift.

#### Impact
Hit **~40 CCU** immediately after release and **revived the game** with a stable ecosystem + near-instant onboarding.
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/Andromeda

featuredDetails.problem: A multiplayer game becomes unplayable when its backend path disappears — especially with **no documentation**, and when mod installs are fragile/manual.
featuredDetails.approach: Reverse-engineer behavior, implement the mod from scratch, ship an Avalonia installer for instant setup, and run dockerized servers + Core REST API on cost-optimized GCP, with CI/CD releases and client update warnings.
featuredDetails.impact: **~5s installs**, strong production stability, immediate adoption (**~40 CCU**), and a foundation to iterate without breaking users.

title: Scribe
summary: Distributed transcription pipeline: Discord capture + pyannote diarization + Whisper transcription + Gemini summaries, with GPU worker nodes for heavy compute and tight Discord integration.

content.text: **Scribe** turns Discord calls into usable artifacts: diarized transcripts + structured summaries — using a distributed architecture so heavy compute runs where it should (**GPU worker nodes**).

#### Pipeline
1. **Discord bot** joins voice channels and captures/uploads jobs.
2. **Server** coordinates jobs and storage.
3. **Worker nodes** pull pending work and run: **pyannote** → **Whisper** → **Gemini**.
4. Results are posted back and tightly integrated with the Discord UX.

featuredDetails.problem: Always-on transcription is operationally annoying: diarization/transcription need GPU power, but coordination needs to be cheap and reliable.
featuredDetails.approach: Split into Discord ingestion + coordinator server + GPU workers, and implement diarization → transcription → summary.
featuredDetails.impact: Always-on capture with high-quality output without paying the “GPU box running 24/7” tax.

title: gocode
summary: A fast Go (chi) backend template with cookie-based JWT auth, Swagger docs, and a practical dev/prod story (SQLite in debug, Postgres in prod).

content.text: **gocode** is a “starter that doesn’t lie”: cookie-based JWT sessions + refresh, Swagger docs, and env-driven config aimed at real deployments.

#### Highlights
- **Cookie JWT auth** (session + refresh cookies)
- **Swagger** documentation generation
- Debug/dev mode: **SQLite**
- Production mode: **Postgres**
- Mailer support + security knobs (rate limits / lockouts / token expiries)
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/gocode

title: Driverly
summary: Multi-tenant driving school platform built with Django + Vite/Vue and PostgreSQL, featuring scheduling/booking + tight Google Calendar + Stripe/PayPal/Square + mailing + full management.

content.text: **Driverly** is a multi-tenant platform for driving schools (**each tenant = a school**) built with **Django + PostgreSQL** and a **Vite/Vue** frontend.

#### Core capabilities
- **Scheduling + booking** built into the platform
- **Google Calendar integration** (tight sync)
- Payments: **Stripe**, **PayPal**, **Square**
- **Mailing/communications** as a first-class feature
- Full admin/management surface area for day-to-day operations

featuredDetails.problem: Schools need a single system that handles scheduling reality + payments + communications across multiple tenants, not a patchwork of tools.
featuredDetails.approach: Build multi-tenancy into the foundation (Django), pair with a modern frontend (Vite/Vue), and integrate deeply with calendar + payment + mailing workflows.
featuredDetails.impact: A scalable operational platform suitable for multiple schools with automation where it matters most.

title: plzstalk
summary: A “Wayback Machine for TikTok”: tracks reposts/videos/likes/followers/following through deep TikTok API reverse engineering. Built with a gocode-style backend + Next.js frontend; supports SQLite too.

content.text: **plzstalk** makes TikTok profiles *observable over time* — effectively a **Wayback Machine** for account activity built from reverse engineered APIs.

#### What it captures
- **Reposts**
- **Videos**
- **Likes**
- **Following / followers** changes

#### Stack
- Backend: **gocode-style Go API**
- Frontend: **Next.js**
- Storage: includes **SQLite** support

title: swcuts.com
summary: Booking platform for a barber shop using a gocode backend + Next.js frontend, supporting multi-barber scheduling, mailing, and a clean booking UX.

content.text: **swcuts.com** is a booking website built for a barber friend — optimized for the small-business reality (simple UX, fast admin flows, multi-barber support).

#### Stack
- Backend: **gocode-based Go API** (auth + mailing patterns)
- Frontend: **Next.js**

title: SStorage
summary: Self-hosted media storage/sharing app with image/video uploads, FFmpeg transcoding, privacy controls, and an admin panel — shipped with Docker Compose and web-based configuration.

content.text: **SStorage** is a self-hosted alternative for media uploads + sharing, designed for direct access (great for embedding), with a real admin story.

#### Features
- Drag-and-drop **uploads** (images + videos)
- **FFmpeg transcoding** + configurable compression
- User accounts + per-user uploads
- Privacy controls (including **force-private** behavior)
- Admin dashboard: stats, user management, global limits, SMTP, defaults
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/sstorage

title: Treenode
summary: Full-stack link-in-bio platform: customizable pages + per-user subdomains, auth, dashboard, and a modern UI — Go backend + Next.js frontend.

content.text: **Treenode** lets users build link pages accessible via **subdomains**, with a dashboard for management and a security/performance-focused backend.

#### Stack
- Backend: **Go (chi)**, cookie JWT, Swagger, rate limiting, SMTP/Resend
- Frontend: **Next.js**, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn/ui
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/treenode

title: Marchive
summary: Anna’s Archive–inspired library app with discovery, queued downloads, admin tooling, and real-time progress — Docker deployment and dev-friendly SQLite mode.

content.text: **Marchive** is a library platform built around large-scale discovery and download workflows (queueing + progress tracking + admin controls).

#### Highlights
- Automated discovery + metadata extraction
- Queue-based download management
- Admin panel + real-time progress
- Docker deployment (compose)
- Dev mode supports **SQLite**
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/marchive

title: Midah
summary: Cross-platform soundboard built with Tauri (Rust + TS): hotkeys, categories, auto-updater, VB-Cable installer, and YouTube audio import via yt-dlp + FFmpeg.

content.text: **Midah** is a lightweight VB-Cable soundboard designed to feel instant: low RAM/CPU, clean organization, and hotkeys so you don’t even need to open the UI.

#### Features
- Cross-platform (Windows + Linux support)
- **Autoupdater**
- Hotkeys + categories
- Optional local-only playback
- **VB-Cable installer**
- **YouTube search/import** (auto-installs `yt-dlp` + `ffmpeg`)
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/midah

title: MRIE
summary: Modular self-hosted platform built with ASP.NET + Identity + SignalR + EF Core featuring an admin config system and multiple apps (AI lab assistant, media tools, prayer times API, URL shortener…).

content.text: **MRIE** is a “platform of tools” meant to replace clunky or proprietary utilities with **developer-friendly, self-hostable** modules.

#### Notable modules
- **Lab AI** assistant workflow
- **Prayer times** scraper + public API
- Media downloader/ripper subsystem
- URL shortener
- Fine-grained auth + admin configuration
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/Mrie

title: mawaqitnode
summary: ESP32 PlatformIO project that fetches accurate prayer times and triggers alarms, with optional OLED + buzzer drivers and modular hardware abstraction.

content.text: **mawaqitnode** runs an embedded prayer-times experience on ESP32, with optional screen + sound output.

#### Highlights
- Modular drivers for hardware components
- Configurable build (disable OLED/sound)
- Serial logging for debugging
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/mawaqitnode

title: RMP API
summary: A small API that fetches RateMyProfessors data with searchable endpoints, optional expanded payloads, and rate limiting.

content.text: **RMP API** provides endpoints for school search and professor lookups, with optional inclusion of ratings/related professors to keep responses small by default.
**Repo:** https://github.com/akramboussanni/rmp-api

title: Honda
summary: Self-hosted Wake-on-LAN + uptime/status management app with Docker Compose deployment, paired with hondakey (ESP32 companion) for remote triggers.

content.text: **Honda** is a web app for managing WOL and home machine status. It’s designed to run via **Docker Compose** and includes dashboard views for monitoring and control.
**hondakey** is the ESP32 companion app used to send WOL packets through the system.

**Repos:**
- Honda: https://github.com/akramboussanni/honda
- hondakey: https://github.com/akramboussanni/hondakey

title: Wirdbot
summary: Structured Python bot/service under the adhdhikr org with Docker support, modular cogs, database + migrations, and a maintainable project layout.

content.text: **Wirdbot** is a Docker-friendly Python bot/service project with a modular structure (**cogs**, **migrations**, DB layer) intended for real operation rather than a single-file script.
**Repo:** https://github.com/adhdhikr/wirdbot

title: Champanzee
summary: Real-time multiplayer survey platform (sub-200ms sync) built at uOttaHack 8 that placed 2nd out of 40+ teams, with Dockerized infra (Go + Redis + Mongo + Next.js) and Gemini-powered features.

content.text: **Champanzee** turns surveys into **live multiplayer game sessions**: hosts create rooms, players join in real time, and answers sync across participants with **sub-200ms latency**.
Built at **[uOttaHack 8](https://2025.uottahack.ca/)** (January 2026) where it placed **2nd out of 40+ teams**.

#### Links
- Website: https://champanzee.tech
- Repo: https://github.com/rbous/Champanzee

featuredDetails.problem: Classic surveys are passive and slow; it’s hard to run engaging sessions and get high-signal responses quickly.
featuredDetails.approach: Turn the survey into a room-based game with real-time sync and a full Dockerized stack, with AI assistance where it improves outcomes.
featuredDetails.impact: A deployable real-time platform that proved itself competitively (2nd / 40+ teams) and demonstrates strong full-stack execution.
